repo_notes.content: UI8Kit is a UI framework combining React developer experience with semantic HTML5/CSS3 output. Dual development model: Development uses React components with full TypeScript; Production targets pure HTML5/CSS3 with semantic class names (Bootstrap/Uikit3-style). All utility props are resolved against `src/lib/utility-props.map.ts` and typically require neither className nor inline style overrides. Semantic class values map to design tokens derived from theme CSS custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, etc.). Critical directories: src/components/ui/ (primitives: Block, Box, Button, Badge, Container, Field, Group, Icon, Image, Stack, Text, Title), src/components/ (composite: Accordion, Card, Grid, Sheet), src/variants/ (CVA definitions: badge, button, card, field, grid, image, typography), src/lib/ (utility-props.ts, utility-props.map.ts, utils.ts). Grid includes Grid.Col subcomponent.
repo_notes.author: UI Systems Architect

repo_notes.content: Vision: Build interactive React apps during development, then generate production-ready static sites with semantic markup. Props-to-CSS mapping enables declarative prop system with semantic output. Component variants provide design system consistency. Static generation pipeline converts React routes to HTML5/CSS3.
repo_notes.author: Framework Vision

repo_notes.content: Minimalism: 18 components and 6 variant modules cover ~80% of design scenarios. Layout primitives (Block, Container, Stack, Group, Box), typography (Title, Text), interactive (Button, Badge, Field), media (Image, Icon), composite (Grid, Card, Sheet, Accordion). Zero-runtime styling, tree shaking, minimal bundle size.
repo_notes.author: Design Philosophy

repo_notes.content: Utility props system: `resolveUtilityClassName()` splits props into utility and rest. Keys in `utility-props.map.ts` map to Tailwind-like classes (e.g. p="2" -> p-2, gap="md" -> gap-4). Semantic aliases: gap md/lg/xl, flex direction, bare-token props (italic="italic" -> italic). No runtime validation; whitelist safety is enforced by generated typings and guard scripts. In production, semantic classes are typically token-anchored (for example `bg="primary"`, `textColor="destructive-foreground"`, `shadow="sm"`, `rounded="xl"`).
repo_notes.author: Utility Props

repo_notes.content: CVA variant system: gridVariants (cols semantic patterns), typographyVariants (fontSize, fontWeight, truncate), buttonVariants, badgeVariants, cardVariants, fieldVariants, imageVariants. Components combine variants with utility props via cn(). Prop forwarding, polymorphic component prop, data-class attributes for DOM targeting.
repo_notes.author: Variant System

repo_notes.content: Integration: npm install @ui8kit/core, or use `npx ui8kit` / `bunx ui8kit@latest` for init/add workflow in Vite React projects. JSON registry (packages/registry) for programmatic access. Exports from src/index.ts, src/components/index.ts. Field component exists for form inputs.
repo_notes.author: Integration

repo_notes.content: ui8kit CLI (`npx ui8kit` / `bunx ui8kit@latest`) is maintained in separate package `ui8kit` from `https://github.com/ui8kit/cli` and published as `https://www.npmjs.com/package/ui8kit`. Commands in this project context include `init`, `add`, `list`, `diff`, `scan`, `build`, `info`, `reset`, `cache clear`, `registry clean`. Global options: --cwd, --verbose, --no-cache. `init`, `add`, `list`, `diff`, `scan`, `build`, and `info` support registry override flags (`--registry-url`, `--registry-version`, `--strict-cdn`). `add` supports dependency ordering and dry-run diff preview.
repo_notes.author: CLI Commands

repo_notes.content: CLI source of truth for LLM navigation is external: `https://github.com/ui8kit/cli` (npm package `ui8kit`). The package separates command entrypoint, command modules, CDN/cache configuration, and import transform behavior in its own repository.
repo_notes.author: CLI Source-of-Truth Map

repo_notes.content: LLM orientation model: first anchor context in DESCRIPTION.md and CLI README, then use repository entrypoints (src/index.ts and src/components/index.ts), utility pipeline (src/lib/utility-props.ts + utility-props.map.ts), and variant layer (src/variants/*). For implementation work, prioritize src/lib and src/components, then cross-check registry/build tooling in packages/registry and CLI workflows in package scripts.
repo_notes.author: LLM Navigation

repo_notes.content: Key features: Type-safe components, semantic CSS class naming, responsive utilities (breakpoint variants), zero-runtime styling, static generation ready, compound components (Card.Header, Accordion.Content, Grid.Col), full TypeScript.
repo_notes.author: Key Features

repo_notes.content: Design token coverage in core is practical-first today; full token parity is expected to be covered by the separate generator CLI flow when shadcn map support is enabled. That pipeline will keep mapping exhaustive and allow token updates without changing consumer component APIs.
repo_notes.author: Platform Roadmap

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Library introduction, dual development model, design philosophy, and key features

page_notes.content: Explain UI8Kit's vision: React dev â†’ semantic HTML5/CSS3 production. Minimalist approach: 18 components, 6 variant modules. Zero-runtime styling, utility-props validation, Bootstrap/Uikit3-style class naming. Use cases: component docs, static sites, prototyping, enterprise apps.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Installation, Tailwind setup, and basic usage

page_notes.content: Two paths: (1) npm install @ui8kit/core and import directly, or (2) `npx ui8kit init --yes` / `bunx ui8kit@latest init --yes`, then `npx ui8kit add button` / `bunx ui8kit@latest add button` (or card). Tailwind content paths, CSS variables. Basic example: Block, Stack, Title, Text, Button, Card, CardHeader, CardContent.

pages.title: CLI Commands
pages.purpose: ui8kit CLI: init, add, list, diff, scan, build, and utilities

page_notes.content: `npx ui8kit` / `bunx ui8kit@latest` commands: init (project setup, ui8kit.config.json, --yes, --registry, --import-style (alias|package)), add (components, --all, --force, --dry-run, --retry), list (--json), diff, cache clear, info, registry clean, reset, scan, build. Global flags: --cwd, --verbose, --no-cache. `add` may create `src/lib`, `src/components`, `src/components/ui`, `src/blocks`, `src/layouts`, `src/variants`.

pages.title: CLI Reference
pages.purpose: Command-level CLI behavior and options matrix

page_notes.content: Global options for all commands: `-c, --cwd <dir>`, `-v, --verbose`, `--no-cache`. `init`: non-interactive mode via `--yes`; interactive prompts for global css and component alias. `init` validates Vite React and React dependency, writes `ui8kit.config.json`, and installs core registry artifacts (`registry:lib` and `registry:variants`) + `src/variants/index.ts` when available. `add`: positional names + `--all`, `--force`, `--retry`, `--dry-run`; installs and sorts dependencies via registry tree; supports registry override flags; `--all` creates install directories (`components`, `lib`, `components/ui`, `blocks`, `layouts`, `variants`). `list`: groups components by registry type; `--json` emits raw registry component list. `diff`: optional component arg; `--json` emits summary. `cache clear`: purges filesystem cache at `~/.ui8kit/cache`. `info`: default human output plus `--cdn` and `--json`; prints cache + CDN resolution state. `registry clean`: clears `packages/registry` artifacts, `--all` additionally removes `src/registry.json`, `--dry-run` only prints paths. `reset`: removes `ui8kit.config.json`, generated component directories, and optional cache with `--with-cache`; supports `--yes/--force/--dry-run`. `scan`: builds `src/registry.json` from local components/variants/lib/blocks/layouts; supports `--source`, `--output`, `--registry`. `build`: builds package registry from `src/registry.json` or path arg; outputs to `packages/registry/r` by default and generates `packages/registry/ui8kit.map.json` when utility props files exist.

pages.title: CLI Config & Registry Model
pages.purpose: How config files, aliases, and CDN overrides affect CLI runtime

page_notes.content: `ui8kit.config.json` schema is validated by zod and includes: `framework`, `typescript`, `globalCss`, `aliases`, `registry`, `componentsDir`, `libDir`, `registryUrl`, `registryVersion`, `strictCdn`, `importStyle`. Defaults: `framework="vite-react"`, `typescript=true`, `globalCss="src/index.css"`, components dir `./src/components`, lib dir `./src/lib`, aliases from `SCHEMA_CONFIG.defaultAliases`. `importStyle=alias` keeps local alias imports, `importStyle=package` rewrites UI imports to `@ui8kit/core`.

pages.title: CLI Data & Runtime Flow
pages.purpose: Where data comes from and how local/remote manifests move through CLI

page_notes.content: Remote flow: `init`/`add` fetch registry through `getCdnUrls` order (`unpkg`, `jsdelivr`, GitHub raw), with optional override URL + version and strict mode. API layer caches index in memory and on disk at `~/.ui8kit/cache` with TTL. Local flow: `scan` analyzes source files and emits `src/registry.json`; `build` validates registry schema, enriches file content from fs, writes versioned artifacts under `packages/registry/r`, and updates `ui8kit.map.json`.

pages.title: CLI Error Glossary
pages.purpose: Common CLI errors and quick recovery steps

page_notes.content: `ConfigNotFoundError`: ui8kit config missing, `add` will fail fast and suggest init. Fix with `npx ui8kit init --yes` / `bunx ui8kit@latest init --yes` or ensure `ui8kit.config.json` exists at project root/src/registry path. `ValidationError` from `handleValidationError`: project not ready or components missing; usually run `init` first, then install dependencies/components in dependency order. `Not a Vite project` / React missing: only Vite React is supported for init/add; install React and add `vite.config.*`.

`No local components found` in `diff`: usually normal on empty projects. Run `add` first or point registry path to an already initialized project. `Component not found`: check exact component name and run with `--dry-run` or `list` to confirm available components.

CDN/network errors (`No working ... CDN found`, registry fetch failures): run with `--retry`, or pass `--registry-url`/`--registry-version` and optionally `--strict-cdn` to control provider list. `--no-cache` is useful when testing newly published registry changes. `checkPrimaryCdn` failures in `info` do not block non-network commands but indicate diagnostics risk.

Dependency install failures during `add`: CLI logs missing/workspace/npm dependencies. Re-run with network available, or install missing packages manually. `--force` is for overwriting files, not bypassing missing registry/validation conditions.

pages.title: CLI Scan & Build Diagnostics
pages.purpose: Typical scan/build issues and what to inspect

page_notes.content: `scan` warnings from `analyzeDeps`: usually indicate unresolved local imports or non-exported files. Fix by ensuring components export something and import paths are valid. If a target folder is missing, scan skips it without failure.

`scan` emits registry component type from directory mapping: ui => `registry:ui`, variants => `registry:variants`, blocks => `registry:block`, layouts => `registry:layout`, lib => `registry:lib`, and composites from `src/components` root/indices as `registry:composite`. If a component appears in unexpected place, check folder structure.

`build` schema failures usually come from `src/registry.json` not matching registry item schema (missing required fields or malformed file lists). Verify JSON from `scan` output and ensure each item has `name`, `type`, and `files` entries.

`build` also writes `ui8kit.map.json` only when `src/lib/utility-props.map.ts` and `src/lib/utility-props.ts` exist and are parseable. If map is missing, check map-source path (`SCHEMA_CONFIG.utilityMap.sources`) and file read/TypeScript parse errors in these files.

When `build` reports missing file from registry artifact path, `src/registry.json` likely references stale `path` values. Re-run `scan` in correct `--source` context, then `build` from repo root or pass accurate `registry` path.

pages.title: CLI Incident Playbooks
pages.purpose: Step-by-step recovery procedures for common CLI failures

page_notes.content:
Playbook: `diff` shows nothing / `No local components found`.
1) Confirm the project has installed components in one of `src/components`, `src/components/ui`, `src/blocks`, `src/layouts`.
2) Run `npx ui8kit add button` / `bunx ui8kit@latest add button` (or another component) and re-run `diff`.
3) If still empty, run `npx ui8kit init --yes` / `bunx ui8kit@latest init --yes` to ensure config and core files exist.
4) Ensure working directory is correct; with subprojects, use `-c <path>`.
5) Re-check `ui8kit.config.json` path via `npx ui8kit info` / `bunx ui8kit@latest info`.

Playbook: `add` reports `Component "X" was not found in registry`.
1) Run `npx ui8kit list --json` / `bunx ui8kit@latest list --json` and confirm the exact component name/case.
2) Re-run `npx ui8kit add` / `bunx ui8kit@latest add` with `--registry-url` and/or `--registry-version` if registry CDN changed.
3) Try `npx ui8kit --no-cache list --json` / `bunx ui8kit@latest --no-cache list --json` to avoid stale registry index.
4) Try with `--retry` on unstable networks.
5) If installed by URL, verify URL returns valid component JSON (`name`, `type`, `files`) and pass exact URL argument to `add`.

Playbook: `scan`/`build` schema errors.
1) Open `src/registry.json` and verify each component has `name`, `type`, `files`, and valid `files[].path`.
2) Regenerate manifest with `npx ui8kit scan --source ./src --output ./src/registry.json` / `bunx ui8kit@latest scan --source ./src --output ./src/registry.json`.
3) Re-run `npx ui8kit build ./src/registry.json` / `bunx ui8kit@latest build ./src/registry.json`.
4) If type errors persist, inspect where the file was picked up (`registry:ui`, `registry:block`, `registry:layout`, `registry:lib`, `registry:variants`, `registry:composite`) and move files to matching folders.
5) For missing dependencies in output, fix source imports before rebuilding.

Playbook: `build` created incomplete registry artifacts.
1) Confirm `src/lib/utility-props.map.ts` and `src/lib/utility-props.ts` exist when expecting `ui8kit.map.json`.
2) Ensure source files referenced by manifest are present and readable from manifest paths.
3) Run `npx ui8kit build --output ./packages/registry/r ./src/registry.json --no-cache` / `bunx ui8kit@latest build --output ./packages/registry/r ./src/registry.json --no-cache` after cleaning cache with `npx ui8kit cache clear` / `bunx ui8kit@latest cache clear`.
4) Open generated files in `packages/registry/r` and `packages/registry/ui8kit.map.json` to verify structure.
5) Keep `schema.json` and `schema/` under `packages/registry` synced after manual edits.

pages.title: CLI Quick Checks
pages.purpose: Short 60-second validation commands before deep troubleshooting

page_notes.content: `init readiness`: `npx ui8kit init --yes` / `bunx ui8kit@latest init --yes` only in Vite React project; then `npx ui8kit info --cdn` / `bunx ui8kit@latest info --cdn` to validate config + CDN status.

`component install path`: `npx ui8kit list --json` / `bunx ui8kit@latest list --json` to confirm availability, then `npx ui8kit add button --dry-run` / `bunx ui8kit@latest add button --dry-run` to verify directory/write planning without mutations.

`cache/state sanity`: `npx ui8kit cache clear` / `bunx ui8kit@latest cache clear`, then `npx ui8kit --no-cache info` / `bunx ui8kit@latest --no-cache info` to confirm fresh lookup and avoid stale CDN/index artifacts.
`local-vs-remote`: `npx ui8kit diff --json` / `bunx ui8kit@latest diff --json` for machine checks; if empty, install at least one component first and rerun.

`registry build sanity`: `npx ui8kit scan --source ./src --output ./src/registry.json` / `bunx ui8kit@latest scan --source ./src --output ./src/registry.json`, then `npx ui8kit build ./src/registry.json --output ./packages/registry/r` / `bunx ui8kit@latest build ./src/registry.json --output ./packages/registry/r`.

pages.title: Build System Quick Checks
pages.purpose: Short verification steps for compilation and artifact generation

page_notes.content: `core type/build check`: run `npm run type-check` and `npm run build` in the package root to verify TypeScript and bundling integrity before touching CLI workflows.

`output location check`: ensure outputs are produced in expected places (`dist/` for package publish artifacts, `packages/registry/r` for registry artifacts, `packages/registry/schema` for schema outputs).
`package json sanity`: confirm required scripts and dependency groups in `package.json` and CLI package scripts are aligned with runtime expectations after dependency updates.
`clean rebuild`: remove temporary caches (`node_modules/.cache` + `~/.ui8kit/cache`), reinstall dependencies, then rerun type-check/build when build outputs look stale or inconsistent.
`pre-commit check`: run tests/lint pipeline used by CI (`npm run test`, `npm run lint`, optional static checks) before publishing or regenerating registry artifacts.

pages.title: LLM Navigation Playbook
pages.purpose: Fast file-first route for answering architecture/CLI questions

page_notes.content: Question: "How does component installation work?" â†’ start from CLI source docs: https://github.com/ui8kit/cli and inspect `add` command flow, then dependency resolver and import transform modules there.

Question: "Why this command failed?" â†’ inspect nearest command implementation in `https://github.com/ui8kit/cli`, then error handling and project config paths in the same repository, then confirm command wiring in its command index.

Question: "Where does registry data come from?" â†’ check external CLI `SCHEMA_CONFIG` and `registry api` flow in `https://github.com/ui8kit/cli`, then compare with corresponding command docs in that repository README.

Question: "How are utility props generated and validated?" â†’ open `src/lib/utility-props.ts` and `src/lib/utility-props.map.ts`, then verify CLI map generation path in `SCHEMA_CONFIG.utilityMap.sources` and `packages/registry/ui8kit.map.json` output.

Question: "Build pipeline broken?" â†’ follow `packages/registry` for distribution expectations in this repo, then scan/build command flow in `https://github.com/ui8kit/cli`, and finally verify generated artifacts in `packages/registry/r`.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Component structure, utility props, variants, and output flow

page_notes.content: React Component â†’ Type validation + utility-props â†’ HTML5/CSS3 Output. Primitives (ui/) vs composite (Accordion, Card, Grid, Sheet). CVA variants + utility-props.map.ts. resolveUtilityClassName, cn() merging.

pages.title: Utility Props
pages.purpose: utility-props.map.ts, resolveUtilityClassName, semantic aliases

page_notes.content: Centralized definition in `utility-props.map.ts`. `ux()` + `resolveUtilityClassName()` build classes via whitelisted token pairs. Special cases: flex direction (`flex`/`col` -> `flex flex-col`), semantic gaps (`md` â†’ `gap-4`), and bare-token aliases (`italic`, `hidden`, etc.). Design intent: layout/spacing primitives (`p`, `m`, `gap`, `flex`, `justify`, `items`) + tokenized styles (`bg`, `text`, `border`, `shadow`, `rounded`) cover common layout/typography/tokening scenarios without custom class/style usage. No runtime class validation in component code.

Theme tokens are defined in global CSS variables and exposed as semantic token classes (`bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border-input`, etc.), so palette and radius/typography/surface changes happen by theme edits, not by component changes. Keep utility updates additive: add new token names to `utility-props.map.ts` and regenerate the utility map only when you introduce new semantic token names. Current practical coverage in this repo is intentionally incremental. Full token parity is planned to be managed by the generator flow in a separate CLI utility (including map generation from shadcn-style token sources), so coverage can be treated as expandable without changing component semantics.

pages.title: Theming & Design Tokens
pages.purpose: How tokenized design variables are consumed by utility props

page_notes.content: Global theme variables provide source of truth for colors, fonts, radius and shadows. The library consumes these through tokenized class names (`bg-*`, `text-*`, `border-*`, `shadow-*`, `rounded-*`) emitted by utility props and variants. This allows switching from light to dark mode and brand palette by changing CSS variables, while component usage (`bg`, `textColor`, `fontSize`, `p`, `gap`, etc.) stays stable.

In practice this means: if styling needs stay within existing tokens (`accent`, `primary`, `muted`, `destructive`, spacing scale, border/shadow presets), you avoid custom styles in component markup entirely. If a new semantic token is introduced, add it to `utility-props.map.ts` and keep generation pipelines updated so it becomes accepted as props.

pages.title: Variant System
pages.purpose: CVA-based variants: grid, typography, button, badge, card, field, image

page_notes.content: src/variants/: grid (cols patterns), typography (fontSize, truncate), button, badge, card, field, image. Components combine variants with utility props. Type-safe VariantProps.

pages.title: Layout Components (Architecture)
pages.purpose: Block, Container, Stack, Group, Box, Grid

page_notes.content: Block (polymorphic), Container (responsive), Stack/Group (flex), Box (universal primitive), Grid (cols number/array/semantic, Grid.Col). All support utility props.

pages.title: UI Components (Architecture)
pages.purpose: UI and interactive components from architecture perspective

page_notes.content: Compound patterns: Card.Header/Content/Footer, Accordion.Item/Trigger/Content. Field for form inputs. Grid.Col. data-class attributes for testing.

pages.title: Package Structure
pages.purpose: package.json, exports, dist/, packages/registry, ui8kit.config.json

page_notes.content: Entry: dist/index.js. Exports from src/index.ts, src/components/index.ts. packages/registry: CDN artifacts for ui8kit add/list/diff. ui8kit.config.json: created by init, stores project config (registry type, import alias, CSS path). CLI-driven commands (`scan`, `build`) are exposed via ui8kit package externally (`npx ui8kit` / `bunx ui8kit@latest`).

pages.title: Build System
pages.purpose: TypeScript compilation, build scripts, dist output

page_notes.content: tsc -p tsconfig.json. Output to dist/. Type definitions, build scripts.

pages.title: Component Registry
pages.purpose: packages/registry, ui8kit.map.json, scan and build workflow

page_notes.content: packages/registry: component metadata, CDN artifacts. scan: scans src/ â†’ registry.json manifest. build: registry.json â†’ packages/registry/r, also generates ui8kit.map.json from utility-props.map.ts + utility-props.ts (flat Tailwind class array). add fetches from registry CDN. list/diff use registry. reset removes packages/registry artifacts.

pages.title: API Reference
pages.purpose: Complete component API: props, types, variants

pages.title: Core Components
pages.purpose: API for Block, Box, Button

page_notes.content: Block (polymorphic), Box (universal), Button (variants). Prop interfaces, forwardRef, component polymorphism.

pages.title: UI Components
pages.purpose: API for Card, Badge, Title, Text, Field, Image, Icon, Grid, Sheet, Accordion

page_notes.content: Full prop APIs. Compound components. Grid.Col (span, start, end, order). Field for form inputs.

pages.title: Layout Components
pages.purpose: API for Container, Stack, Group, Grid

page_notes.content: Container, Stack, Group, Grid. Grid cols: number, array of {bp, value}, or semantic string (1-3, etc).

pages.title: Development Guide
pages.purpose: Developer workflows, testing, best practices

page_notes.content: Component development, testing (vitest), lint (eslint). Extending variants, utility props usage.

pages.title: Component Development
pages.purpose: Creating custom components, extending variants

page_notes.content: Extend base components, create variants, add to utility-props.map. resolveUtilityClassName pattern.

pages.title: Best Practices
pages.purpose: Semantic props, data-class, accessibility

page_notes.content: Prefer semantic props over className. data-class for targeting. Utility props over inline styles.

pages.title: Static Generation
pages.purpose: React to HTML5/CSS3 conversion, future roadmap

page_notes.content: Roadmap: React routes â†’ HTML files, preserve semantic structure, SEO-optimized output. Props-to-CSS mapping enables static generation.

pages.title: Troubleshooting
pages.purpose: Common issues, debugging

page_notes.content: Styling issues, TypeScript errors, utility props validation, form handling with Field.
